title: Parts of Speech
subject: English
objectives: Identify the eight parts of speech
Understand the function of each in a sentence

sections.heading: The eight parts of speech
sections.content:
Every word in English belongs to at least one of eight categories called parts of speech. Understanding them is essential for writing clearly and fixing grammatical errors.

1. Noun: a person, place, thing, or idea (dog, Paris, freedom)
2. Pronoun: replaces a noun (he, she, it, they, who)
3. Verb: an action or state of being (run, think, is)
4. Adjective: describes a noun (tall, blue, three)
5. Adverb: describes a verb, adjective, or another adverb (quickly, very, often)
6. Preposition: shows relationships between words (in, on, between, during)
7. Conjunction: connects words or groups of words (and, but, because)
8. Interjection: expresses emotion (wow, ouch, hey)

sections.heading: Identifying parts of speech in a sentence
sections.content:
Consider: 'The clever student quickly solved the difficult problem.'

• The — article (a type of adjective)
• clever — adjective (describes 'student')
• student — noun (person)
• quickly — adverb (describes how the student solved)
• solved — verb (action)
• the — article
• difficult — adjective (describes 'problem')
• problem — noun (thing)

Notice that a word's part of speech depends on how it's used. 'Run' is a verb in 'I run daily' but a noun in 'I went for a run.'
The eight parts of speech are the building blocks of English grammar. A word's category depends on its function in the sentence, not just the word itself.

aiContext: This lesson covers the eight parts of speech at a high school English level. Students learn to identify nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, prepositions, conjunctions, and interjections.
